theme: alchemist mixing ratios and potion yields
title: The Alchemist's Limiting Reagent — recovering integer stoichiometry from noisy brews
strategies: constant baseline: predict the mean training yield (ignores reagents entirely) -> ~0.1

ordinary least squares y ~ 1 + q1 + q2 + q3 + q4: fits the near-balanced training cluster (where every reagent moves together) but extrapolates as a SUM of positive contributions, so it wildly over-predicts yield once one reagent becomes truly scarce

integer-lattice snap: enumerate small coprime stoichiometric vectors s in {1..6}^4, fit the single gain g by closed-form least squares against min_i(q_i/s_i) for each candidate, keep the s minimising training SSE, and emit the exact min-law expression
alternatives: wider integer search range, robust/median-based g estimation, or a coarse-to-fine local search around the best lattice point
